evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: fast-paced environment; ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed framing: one velocity signal (fast-paced) paired with multiple rigour signals (accuracy, integrity, consistency, data quality emphasis).
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: SaaS benefits platform serving employee data; errors affect business decisions but not regulatory/financial reporting, placing it at low-to-moderate risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: data engineers; business teams; data analysts
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and maintain BI-ready datasets; data transformation frameworks; data models
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Airflow) in responsibilities and multiple data roles implied suggest mid-stage team with growing infrastructure.
evidence.jd_authorship: collaborate with data engineers; translate requirements into data models; ensure business users have access to trusted data sources
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Generic boilerplate language typical of recruiter authorship—could describe many data roles without specific context or volume signals.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: data analysts and business stakeholders; business teams; business intelligence
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Roles serve both analytics (internal data) and business-driven reporting without strong commercial/finance emphasis.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Execution-focused language (support, collaborate, provide) without strategic direction-setting or priority-defining verbs.
evidence.ai_role: Build and iterate on AI/ML models such as churn prediction and forecasting, from feature preparation through to deployment
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Candidate expected to prepare data and build infrastructure for AI/ML systems downstream (feature engineering, model-ready datasets).
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across all reporting layers; Maintain comprehensive documentation
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality/integrity is named responsibility without explicit testing frameworks or tool ownership.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensure high-quality, well-structured data; data accuracy, integrity, and consistency
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: data quality and integrity are concerns but framed within normal operational delivery, not as risk control or compliance.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and maintain; Develop and optimize; Optimize SQL-based transformations
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Emphasis on optimization and maintenance of existing systems rather than building from zero.
evidence.language_gate: Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
